microsoft_ft: Managed a **complex safety-adjacent validation program** across Azure's full resilience stack, synthesizing inputs from engineering, product, legal, and enterprise stakeholders to drive $14M+ in business impact across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining requirements and self-service scheduling infrastructure that reduced operational toil by 30% and established a scalable, repeatable execution model across 45+ annual drills.

Designed and executed a proactive rack-level drill program end-to-end in 4 months, achieving a **94% recovery rate** and surfacing critical hardware defects — establishing a new continuous validation model adopted across Azure's infrastructure org.

Directed **14 high-stakes cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including acting as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, managing risk tables and stakeholder alignment throughout.

Owned compute and capacity planning for resilience infrastructure, building prioritization frameworks to resource top-priority programs while identifying opportunities for key infrastructure investments across partner and sovereign environments.

Scaled team leverage through **AI-driven automation**, building an internal AI agent and restructuring planning workflows to cut planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill throughput capacity by 21% without adding headcount.

Institutionalized incident management processes and mitigation update cadences across the resilience program, creating structured escalation paths and cross-functional review loops that ensured risks were effectively monitored and resolved at every stage.
microsoft_2023: Drove **AI-powered tooling adoption** across 14 Azure service teams by designing and delivering targeted demos and training programs, embedding AI-driven code generation workflows into engineering pipelines and saving 37 engineering hours monthly.

Facilitated **11+ structured user research sessions** with Azure engineering teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing the product roadmap to prioritize intent-based YAML generation as a core infrastructure capability.

Strengthened **data integrity and retrieval infrastructure** by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation systems to an AI-powered semantic search tool, reducing lookup time by 83% and improving signal quality for downstream workflows.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and reduced region launch timelines by 28% by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ teams — synthesizing competing stakeholder inputs into a single executable roadmap.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to **quantify 81 hours of manual operational toil** per region launch, then built and socialized a phased roadmap to automate critical paths, directly reducing risk and resource burden across the infrastructure portfolio.

Engineered a **Power BI data infrastructure dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to surface automation gaps, prioritize high-impact investments, and make evidence-based capacity planning decisions at scale.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating risk mitigation across IT, Operations, and Engineering stakeholders.

Managed **backlog prioritization and incident triage** across Agile ceremonies — including sprint planning and retrospectives — resolving 40+ high-priority tickets and stabilizing the developer intake process to unblock critical program milestones.

Drove cross-functional alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a scalable deployment model for future robotic system rollouts.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals through a structured CRM process, reducing friction and improving conversion at every stage of the sales pipeline.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversion rates by 2.7% by executing a data-informed digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and local search profiles to improve top-of-funnel performance.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying and sealing margin leaks that had gone undetected and directly informing updated pricing models across service lines.

tailoring_notes: microsoft_ft rewritten to foreground safety-stack program management, risk table ownership, stakeholder synthesis across research/product/legal/policy, compute/capacity planning, and incident management — all direct JD language mirrors.
Added a 7th bullet to microsoft_ft to explicitly surface incident management process design and mitigation update cadences, which are called out verbatim in the JD's core responsibilities.

microsoft_2023 reframes AI tooling adoption and semantic search work as data infrastructure and AI-powered safety tooling experience, signaling familiarity with the technical surface area OpenAI cares about.

microsoft_2022 emphasizes cross-functional synthesis, stakeholder alignment across large orgs, and data infrastructure dashboarding — directly paralleling the JD's compute/data infrastructure and portfolio prioritization responsibilities.

amazon_robotics highlights risk mapping, dependency management, incident triage, and cross-org alignment — framed to resonate with OpenAI's need for a TPM who can manage complex technical risk across engineering and ops stakeholders.
Skills section reordered to lead with TPM, safety/risk, and stakeholder synthesis before technical tools, matching the JD's emphasis on program leadership over pure engineering.
All bullets target 250–290 chars with substantive JD-aligned detail; filler phrases avoided in favor of methodology, scope, and outcome framing that mirrors OpenAI's vocabulary.
